channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Wildfires devastate southern Europe as climate crisis intensifies
item.description: In July 2026, unprecedented wildfires have erupted across Portugal, Spain, and Greece, driven by record-breaking heatwaves and prolonged drought. The fires, which have scorched thousands of hectares, ...

item.title: IMF's Focus on Debt Repayment Concerns Kenya's Public Spending
item.description: A new report by ActionAid, Education International, and the Tax and Education Alliance criticizes the IMF for prioritizing debt repayment and fiscal austerity over investments in public services like ...

item.title: Tanzania and Angola Strengthen Economic Ties Through Cooperation
item.description: Tanzania and Angola are enhancing their diplomatic relationship by prioritizing economic cooperation, aiming to expand investment opportunities, trade partnerships, and industrial collaboration. This ...

item.title: Legal challenge over national dialogue appointments as nominees seek court intervention
item.description: A legal dispute has emerged regarding the formal appointment process for participants in President Cyril Ramaphosa's national dialogue initiative. Eight applicants, including Gordon Lesley Rolls and P...

item.title: Jude Bellingham's Stellar Performance Leads England to Victory Over Mexico in World Cup Match
item.description: In a high-stakes encounter at the Azteca Stadium, England secured a crucial victory against Mexico in the 2026 World Cup qualifiers. Jude Bellingham emerged as the standout performer, delivering a mas...

item.title: Preliminary hearing begins for Tyler Robinson in Charlie Kirk murder case
item.description: Tyler Robinson, a 23-year-old man accused of assassinating Turning Point USA founder Charlie Kirk at Utah Valley University, is set for a week-long preliminary hearing to determine if prosecutors have...

item.title: Aero Contractors urges enhanced public-private partnership for aviation growth
item.description: Captain Ado Sanusi, MD/CEO of Aero Contractors, emphasized the need for closer collaboration between the Nigerian government and private sector operators in the aviation industry. During a visit by Sp...

item.title: Pizza Express Investigation Finds No Evidence of Andrew's Woking Visit
item.description: The article details how Pizza Express conducted an internal inquiry into a claim made by Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor regarding his alleged visit to a Woking branch in 2001. The inquiry, prompted by BBC...

item.title: Chinabank allows users to access upcoming payslip funds
item.description: Chinabank has introduced a new service called 'Anti-petsa de peligro' (which translates to 'Anti-Danger Date') that allows customers to access their upcoming payslip funds before their regular payday....

item.title: Madhya Pradesh reconstitutes Waqf Board under amended law, includes two Hindu members
item.description: The Madhya Pradesh government has reconstituted its 10-member Waqf Board under the provisions of the Waqf (Amendment) Act, 2025, marking a significant development in the administration of Waqf propert...

item.title: Arrest of Mike Defensor, Espiritu, and Marcoleta in P75-M Plunder Case
item.description: Former Anakalusugan party-list Rep. Michael Defensor and businessman Joseph Varias Espiritu, co-accused of Senator Rodante Marcoleta in the P75-million plunder case, were arrested by Interior Secretar...

item.title: Opec+ raises output targets as global oil supplies recover
item.description: Oil prices declined slightly after Opec+ agreed to increase output targets starting August, with Gulf exports through the Strait of Hormuz recovering. Brent crude fell 34 cents to $71.78 per barrel, w...

item.title: Panel Show 'Unacceptable' Criticized for Unnecessary and Controversial Content
item.description: The article reviews the BBC's panel show 'Unacceptable,' hosted by Ed Gamble and featuring comedians defending their worst opinions. The show, produced by Romesh Ranganathan's company, is criticized f...

item.title: Albanese signs Australia-Fiji defense pact amid regional security shifts
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has finalized a significant security agreement with Fiji, marking a strategic move against Chinese influence in the Indo-Pacific region. The deal, announced during his ...

item.title: Managing a $100,000 Windfall: Financial Advice for Canadians
item.description: The article explores how to wisely manage a $100,000 inheritance, using the story of George, a Toronto-based IT manager, as a case study. Financial experts advise against impulsive spending, emphasizi...

item.title: Flight Dispatcher Salaries and Benefits in Kenya
item.description: The article details the salary ranges and benefits for flight dispatchers in Kenya. Entry-level dispatchers earn between Ksh 70,000 and Ksh 120,000, mid-level between Ksh 120,000 and Ksh 180,000, whil...

item.title: Octogenarian Shares Experience of Unwanted Compliments on Beauty
item.description: In a letter to Miss Manners, an octogenarian woman recounts her surprise and discomfort at receiving frequent compliments about her appearance from strangers. Despite being in her 80s, she has never c...

item.title: NYSC Reform and the Need for National Dialogue
item.description: The article discusses the proposed reforms for the National Youth Service Corps (NYSC), emphasizing the need for a national dialogue to address its transformation into a skills-focused development pro...

item.title: Spur Franchise Faces Labour Abuse Allegations in Cape Town
item.description: Workers at a Spur franchise in Cape Town have accused the employer of exploitative practices, including poverty wages, excessive working hours, and denial of basic workplace rights. Employees allege t...

item.title: Police Say Suspects Arrested as Investigations Continue into Alleged Plans for Violent Protests
